data.title: YETI outlines 2025 strategy with 30 new product launches and accelerated supply chain transformation
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: YETI Holdings, Inc. (YETI) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Matt Reintjes highlighted three priorities for 2025: accelerating product innovation, transforming the supply chain to minimize reliance on China, and maintaining operational discipline to sustain a strong balance sheet. Reintjes emphasized the impact of supply chain diversification efforts, stating that 90% of U.S. drinkware production will be ex-China by year-end.
  • Management reported a record pipeline of over 30 new products planned for release in 2025, surpassing the 24 products launched in 2024. Innovations include insulated sports jugs, beach chairs, and food-related products.
  • CFO Mike McMullen noted sales growth of 3% to $351 million in Q1, driven by strong performance in Coolers & Equipment and international markets. He also mentioned a significant decline in SG&A expenses compared to internal projections due to cost management efforts.
  • Reintjes expressed confidence in long-term growth despite current macro challenges, stating that YETI is focusing on strategic investments such as new testing and innovation centers in Asia and Denver.

Outlook

  • Management expects full-year sales growth of 1%-4% for 2025, incorporating a 300-basis-point impact from supply chain disruptions as drinkware production is transitioned out of China.
  • Gross margins for 2025 are projected at approximately 54%, reflecting the impact of tariffs. Adjusted EPS guidance was revised to $1.96-$2.02, down from prior expectations of $2.90-$2.95, driven by supply disruptions and tariff costs.
  • International growth is projected at 15%-20%, with strong contributions anticipated from Europe and Japan, where commercial sales are set to begin in Q2 2025.

Financial Results

  • Coolers & Equipment sales grew 17% year-over-year, reaching $140.2 million in Q1. This marks the fifth consecutive quarter of double-digit growth for this category.
  • Drinkware sales decreased by 4% to $205.6 million due to supply chain challenges and a competitive U.S. market. However, international drinkware sales achieved double-digit growth.
  • Direct-to-consumer (DTC) sales rose 4% to $196.2 million, bolstered by strong performance on Amazon and in corporate sales.
  • Inventory levels decreased by 9% year-over-year, reflecting strategic management amid supply chain disruptions. The company ended Q1 with $259 million in cash.

Q&A

  • Randy Konik, Jefferies: Asked about the cadence of new product launches and their reception compared to prior years. Reintjes responded that YETI’s innovation pace will increase, with over 30 new products planned for 2025, supported by supply chain transitions and operational improvements.
  • Brooke Roach, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about the outlook for the drinkware business and wholesale demand. Reintjes explained that the company is diversifying the drinkware portfolio to mitigate market concentration risks and noted strong wholesale partnerships.
  • Peter Benedict, Baird: Questioned pricing strategies amid tariff impacts. Reintjes stated that the primary mitigation strategy is accelerating supply chain transitions, with targeted pricing adjustments to manage costs effectively.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about supply chain disruptions and tariff-related cost pressures. Questions focused on inventory management, product launches, and competitive dynamics in the drinkware market.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing long-term growth opportunities and strategic investments. However, a more cautious tone emerged during discussions of tariff impacts and consumer demand uncertainty.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone appeared more defensive, particularly regarding revised guidance and supply chain challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Sales growth slowed to 3% in Q1 2025 from 7% in Q4 2024, attributed to drinkware supply chain issues and a competitive market.
  • Adjusted EPS guidance for 2025 was significantly reduced, reflecting tariff impacts and delayed product launches, compared to the optimistic outlook in Q4 2024.
  • Management’s tone shifted to a focus on navigating near-term challenges, contrasting with the more expansive growth narrative in the prior quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted ongoing supply chain disruptions, particularly in drinkware, as a key risk for 2025. Efforts to mitigate these include diversifying production and decreasing reliance on China.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the competitive drinkware market and potential consumer demand softness in the U.S.
  • Tariff impacts remain a significant headwind, with $100 million in gross tariff costs projected for 2025.

Final Takeaway

YETI Holdings is navigating a transitional year in 2025, marked by aggressive supply chain transformations, a record number of new product launches, and strategic investments in global expansion. While the company faces headwinds from tariffs and supply disruptions, its focus on innovation and operational discipline positions it for long-term growth. Investors should monitor the execution of supply chain changes and the market reception to new products, particularly in the competitive drinkware segment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nestle hires adviser for water unit sale - Reuters
data.description:
Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY) hired Rothschild to advise on the sale of its water unit, which could be valued at more than 5 billion euros ($5.6B), in a sale later this year. 

Nestle would like to retain a stake in the water business that includes brands such as Perrier and S.Pellegrino, according to a Reuters report  on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

Private equity shops including Blackstone (BX), Platinum Equity and One Rock Capital have had some preliminary talks about potential offers for the water unit, according to the report. Other parties expressing interest include PAI Partners and CD&R. 

Rothschild, Nestle, PAI, CD&R and Blackstone declined to comment to Reuters. One Rock and Platinum didn't respond to requests for comment.

data.title: Kyndryl rises after analysts remain positive on growth prospects
data.description:
Shares of Kyndryl (NYSE:KD) jumped about 6% on Thursday following its fourth-quarter results and outlook for fiscal 2026 which saw largely positive views from analysts.

J.P. Morgan has an Overweight rating and $45 price target on Kyndryl's stock. 

“The clear takeaway is that KD has achieved growth, it expects to grow again this year, and investors must award a greater probability to the case that KD achieves sustainable growth hereafter,” said analysts at J.P. Morgan.

Oppenheimer has an Outperform rating and a price target of $43. Analysts at Oppenheimer said the company achieved an important milestone in returning to constant-currency revenue growth and that the outlook is “solid.”

Scotiabank has a Sector Outperform rating with a $45 price target. “The KD team has once again delivered in line with market expectations, executing ambitious targets and demonstrating considerable progress,” said analysts at Scotiabank. The analysts added that the company is “well-positioned for long-term steady growth as enterprises increasingly prioritize infrastructure modernization.”

Outlook:

For fiscal 2026, the company noted that earnings and cash flow outlook only assumes constant-currency revenue growth of 1%. Kyndryl expects adjusted pretax income at least $725M, a year-over-year increase of at least $243M. The company projects adjusted free cash flow of about $550M and adjusted EBITDA margin of about 18%, a year-over-year increase of around 130 basis points.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades: PLTR, SMCI, BAM, APP, EVGO
data.description:
Here are the latest analyst rating upgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts: 

Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR). Sell to buy by Dhierin Bechai. "Despite PLTR (NASDAQ:PLTR) stock being overvalued, Palantir's (PLTR) growth potential in data analytics and defense solutions makes it a speculative buy for visionary investors."

Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI). Sell to buy by Tech Stock Pros. "We expect SMCI (NASDAQ:SMCI) stock to bounce back after the third cut in guidance in three months and recommend investors load up under $30 per share."

Brookfield Asset Management (NYSE:BAM). Sell to hold by Daniel Urbina. "BAM (NYSE:BAM) stock is notably overvalued against peers, and despite strong financial results, the stock is worth a Hold rating."

AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP). Sell to hold by KM Capital. "Relying on the after-earnings FOMO effect might be too optimistic in the current uncertain environment that spooks investors, meaning that APP (NASDAQ:APP) is a Hold."

EVgo (NASDAQ:EVGO). Sell to hold by Stone Fox Capital. "Investors can consider a speculative bet on EVgo (NASDAQ:EVGO) at 2.5x 2025 sales targets, but sustained growth at scale is crucial for long-term confidence."

data.title: Mosaic upgraded at RBC Capital on strength in phosphate market
data.description:
The Mosaic Co. (NYSE:MOS) late Wednesday was upgraded to Outperform from a previous investment rating of Sector Perform by analysts at RBC Capital Markets. They said tightening phosphate markets and operational tailwinds position the fertilizer giant for renewed earnings momentum.

In a May 7 note to clients, RBC analyst Andrew Wong said Mosaic (NYSE:MOS) is trading at a discount relative to its growth potential, noting the stock currently sits at just 4 to 4.5 times forward 12-month spot earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization. He said he expects tightening global supply and steady demand to continue supporting strong phosphate pricing, while Mosaic (MOS) benefits from several upcoming catalysts.

Phosphate supply remains constrained

“We expect phosphate markets to remain tight given steady demand growth and limited supply,” Wong wrote.

He highlighted limited global supply additions outside of modest increases from Saudi Arabia and Morocco, offset by ongoing restrictions on Chinese exports aimed at securing domestic supply.

In the U.S., Mosaic (MOS) stands to benefit further from trade protections. Tariffs and countervailing duties are keeping the U.S. market especially tight, Wong said, prompting RBC to raise its 2025 and 2026 price forecasts for diammonium phosphate at the NOLA hub by approximately 5%.

Potash markets also resilient

While Wong sees some potential softening in potash prices in the second half of 2025 due to easing North American demand and improved supply, he believes the downside is limited. Strong global demand, particularly from China where inventories remain low, should support prices in the $325–$350 per tonne range, he said.

Operational support on the horizon

RBC expects Mosaic’s (MOS) operations to show notable improvement over the next six to 12 months. These include higher phosphate output, lower per-tonne costs as production scales, and increased distribution volumes in Brazil with the ramp-up of a new blending facility in the second half of the year.

“Management has set out clear plans for operational improvements that if executed well should meaningfully benefit both margins and sales volumes,” Wong said.

The Esterhazy mine is also expected to contribute more low-cost potash production, reducing the company’s cost base over time.

Free cash flow and strategic monetization

Mosaic’s (MOS) capital efficiency is expected to improve as capex declines and pricing remains robust. RBC forecasts $900 million in free cash flow for 2025 and $1.2 billion for 2026 -- representing yields of 9% and 12%, respectively.

Wong also sees potential for further value creation through asset sales.

“We anticipate continued monetization of non-core assets, including idle phosphate facilities in Brazil, the Carlsbad potash mine, and possible early partial divestment” of Mosaic’s (MOS) stake in Ma’aden, he wrote.

RBC raised its 2025 and 2026 ebitda estimates to $2.9 billion and $2.8 billion, respectively, up from previous estimates of $2.6 billion and $2.4 billion.

The bank raised its price target on Mosaic (MOS) shares to $40 a share from $30 a share previously, based on a sum-of-the-parts analysis and enterprise value-to-ebitda ratios.

data.title: Groupon raises billings growth target to 3-5% for FY 2025 with enhanced focus on top cities
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Groupon, Inc. (GRPN) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Dusan Senkypl highlighted that Q1 results exceeded guidance on billings, adjusted EBITDA, and showed a slight beat on revenue. The global billings grew by 1.4% year-over-year, with North America Local accelerating to 11% growth, marking the first double-digit growth in this segment since 2017.
  • The company’s "Things to Do" vertical reported double-digit billings growth for the fifth consecutive quarter, significantly outperforming industry trends.
  • The international local business, excluding Italy, achieved a 5% year-over-year increase in billings, driven by strong performance in key markets like Spain, Germany, the UK, and France.
  • Groupon announced multiple leadership changes, including the appointments of Josef Buryan as Chief Marketing Officer, Ales Drabek as Chief Technology Officer, Barbara Weisz as Chief Revenue Officer, Filip Popovic as Chief Commercial Officer, and Marie Havlickova as Chief Product Officer.
  • CFO Jiri Ponrt noted that the company completed the sale of its Giftcloud subsidiary, removing $6 million in revenue and $4 million in adjusted EBITDA from future consolidated results while maintaining revenue and EBITDA guidance for 2025.

Outlook

  • Groupon raised its 2025 billings growth guidance from 2-4% to 3-5%, citing confidence in its core business performance.
  • Management reiterated that the focus remains on improving revenue and adjusted EBITDA while maintaining stability in the core business despite external challenges.
  • The company expects further acceleration in year-over-year growth for billings and revenue in Q2 2025.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 metrics included growth in gross billings and adjusted EBITDA exceeding previous guidance. Revenue slightly surpassed expectations, though specific figures were not disclosed.
  • North America Local delivered 11% year-over-year billings growth, showcasing the success of Groupon’s hyper-local strategy.
  • Increased focus on higher-quality merchants led to a 43% year-over-year rise in the number of North American merchants generating over $1 million in billings.

Q&A

  • Bobby Brooks, Northland Capital: Asked about the increase in merchants exceeding $1 million in billings. CEO Senkypl credited the hyper-geo approach, focusing on inventory quality and deal performance.
  • Eric Sheridan, Goldman Sachs: Inquired about macroeconomic trends and their impact on merchant pipeline. CEO Senkypl stated that the current environment is a tailwind, enabling deeper partnerships with enterprise and local clients.
  • Sean McGowan, ROTH Capital: Queried about international market updates. CEO Senkypl highlighted Spain’s strong double-digit growth and positive trends in Germany, UK, and France.
  • Analysts also explored the impact of AI integration, with CEO Senkypl elaborating on plans to enhance sales processes, deal optimization, and compatibility with AI-driven search engines.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on operational improvements and macroeconomic tailwinds, but raised detailed questions on marketing ROI and international expansion timelines.
  • Management displayed confidence in its strategic initiatives, emphasizing progress in platform modernization and deal quality. CEO Senkypl struck a positive tone, highlighting significant improvements across key markets.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Billings growth accelerated in Q1 2025 compared to Q4 2024, particularly in North America Local.
  • Guidance for billings growth increased from 2-4% to 3-5%, reflecting stronger-than-expected performance.
  • Leadership changes and international market strategies showed continuity, with Spain maintaining significant growth momentum.
  • Management’s tone was more assertive this quarter, reflecting confidence in the company’s transformation efforts and growth potential.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged deliberate compression in take rates as a short-term revenue headwind but stressed its importance for long-term marketplace health.
  • Analysts questioned the scalability of Groupon’s hyper-local and category-specific strategies across all markets.
  • Concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties and their impact on consumer spending and merchant activity were addressed cautiously.

Final Takeaway

Groupon delivered a strong start to 2025, exceeding guidance on key metrics and raising its billings growth target for the year. The company’s focus on quality over quantity, supported by leadership changes and strategic initiatives, is driving growth in key markets. With ongoing platform enhancements and AI integration, Groupon aims to strengthen its position as a leader in local experiences, signaling confidence in long-term sustainable growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
